sections.heading: Syntax
sections.body:
**S3:**

S3 Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, bucket: <bucket-name>, additionalStorageClasses: [ <storage-classes> ], delimiter: <delimiter>, public: true|false })
```

**Azure:**

Azure Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, serviceURL: <service-account-URL>, containerName: <storage-container-name>, delimiter: <delimiter>, public: true|false })
```

**Gcp:**

GCP Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, bucket: <bucket-name>, delimiter: <delimiter>, prefix: <prefix> })
```

**Atlas:**

Atlas Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, clusterName: <cluster-name>, projectId: <project-id>, readPreference: {mode: <read-preference-mode>, tagSets: [[{name: <name>, value: <value>},...],...], maxStalenessSeconds: <number-of-seconds>}, readConcern: {level: <level>} })
```

**Http:**

HTTP Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, allowInsecure: true|false, urls: [ <url> ], defaultFormat: <file-extension> })
```

**Oa:**

Atlas Online Archive Configuration

```sh
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: "dls:aws", region: <online-archive-region> })
```

codeBlocks.content: db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, bucket: <bucket-name>, additionalStorageClasses: [ <storage-classes> ], delimiter: <delimiter>, public: true|false })

db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, serviceURL: <service-account-URL>, containerName: <storage-container-name>, delimiter: <delimiter>, public: true|false })
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, region: <region-name>, bucket: <bucket-name>, delimiter: <delimiter>, prefix: <prefix> })

db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, clusterName: <cluster-name>, projectId: <project-id>, readPreference: {mode: <read-preference-mode>, tagSets: [[{name: <name>, value: <value>},...],...], maxStalenessSeconds: <number-of-seconds>}, readConcern: {level: <level>} })
db.runCommand({ createStore: <store-name>, provider: <storage-provider>, allowInsecure: true|false, urls: [ <url> ], defaultFormat: <file-extension> })

sections.heading: Parameters
sections.body:
**S3:**

S3 Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| region                   | string           | Region in which the bucket is hosted. For a list of valid region names, see Amazon Web Services (AWS).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | yes |
| ------------------------ | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --- |
| bucket                   | string           | Name of the bucket in which data is stored. Must exactly match the name of an S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket that Data Federation can access using AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) credentials.                                                                                                                                                                                      | yes |
| additionalStorageClasses | array of strings | Array of AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) storage classes. Atlas Data Federation will include the files in these storage classes in the query results. Valid values are:INTELLIGENT\_TIERING to include files in the Intelligent Tiering storage class.STANDARD\_IA to include files in the Standard-Infrequent Access storage class.Files in the Standard storage class are supported by default. | no  |
| delimiter                | string           | Character used to separate path segments in the federated database instance store. If ommitted, defaults to "/".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | no  |
| public                   | boolean          | Flag that indicates whether or not the bucket is public. Valid values are:true to not use the AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role to access the bucketfalse to require the AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role to access the bucketIf omitted, defaults to false.                                                                                       | no  |

**Azure:**

Azure Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| region        | string  | Region in which the container is hosted. For a list of valid region names, see Microsoft Azure.                                                                                                                                                                                                     | yes |
| ------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --- |
| bucket        | string  | URL (Uniform Resource Locator) of the Azure Blob Storage account that contains your blob containers. For example:https\://\<azure-storage-account-name>.blob.core.windows.net/                                                                                                                      | yes |
| containerName | string  | Name of the Azure Blob Storage container that contains the files. For more information, see Azure (Microsoft Azure) documentation for Naming and Referencing Containers.                                                                                                                            | yes |
| delimiter     | string  | Character used to separate path segments in the federated database instance store. If omitted, defaults to "/".                                                                                                                                                                                     | no  |
| public        | boolean | Flag that indicates whether or not the Azure Blob Storage container is public. Valid values are:true to not use the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal to access the bucketfalse to require the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal to access the bucketIf omitted, defaults to false. | no  |

**Gcp:**

GCP Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| region    | string  | Region in which the bucket is hosted. For a list of valid region names, see Google Cloud Platform (GCP).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | yes |
| --------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --- |
| bucket    | string  | Name of the bucket in which data is stored. Must exactly match the name of an Google Cloud Storage bucket that Data Federation can access using the configured Google Cloud IAM (Identity and Access Management) credentials.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | yes |
| delimiter | string  | Character used to separate path segments in the federated database instance store. If ommitted, defaults to "/".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | no  |
| prefix    | boolean | Prefix Atlas Data Federation applies when searching for files in the Google Cloud Storage bucket. For example, consider a Google Cloud Storage bucket metrics with the following structure:metrics \|--hardware \|--software \|--computedThe federated database instance store prepends the value of prefix to databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources.\[n].path to create the full path for files to ingest. Setting prefix to /software restricts any databases objects using the federated database instance to only subpaths of /software.Defaults to the root of the Google Cloud Storage bucket, retrieving all files. | no  |

**Atlas:**

Atlas Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| clusterName                        | string           | Name of the Atlas cluster on which the store is based.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | yes |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --- |
| projectId                          | string           | Unique identifier of the project that contains the Atlas cluster. Atlas Data Federation only supports the projectId of the project where the federated database instance exists. If not specified, Atlas Data Federation assumes that the cluster is located in the same project as the federated database instance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | no  |
| readPreference                     | document         | Cluster read preference, which describes how to route read requests to the cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | no  |
| readPreference.mode                | string           | Read preference mode that specifies which replica set member to route the read requests to. Value can be one of the following:primary - to route all read requests to the replica set primaryprimaryPreferred - to route all read requests the replica set primary and to secondary members only if primary is unavailablesecondary - to route all read requests to the secondary members of the replica setsecondaryPreferred - to route all read requests to the secondary members of the replica set and the primary on sharded clusters only if secondary members are unavailablenearest - to route all read requests to random eligible replica set member, irrespective of whether that member is a primary or secondary | no  |
| readPreference.tagSets             | array of strings | Arrays of tag sets or tag specification documents that contain name and value pairs for the replica set member. If specified, Atlas Data Federation routes read requests to replica set member or members that are associated with the specified tags. To learn more, see Read Preference Tag Sets.Atlas Data Federation doesn't support this parameter for sharded clusters.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | no  |
| readPreference.maxStalenessSeconds | int              | Maximum replication lag, or "staleness", for reads from secondaries. To learn more about maxStalenessSeconds, see Read Preference maxStalenessSeconds.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | no  |

**Http:**

HTTP Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| allowInsecure | boolean                            | Validates the scheme in the specified URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s. Value can be one of the following:true to allow insecure HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) schemefalse to only allow secure HTTPS (Secure HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme (default)If true, Atlas Data Federation:Does not verify the server's certificate chain and hostname.Accepts any certificate with any hostname presented by the server.WARNING: If you set this to true, your data might become vulnerable to a man-in-the-middle attack, which can compromise the confidentiality and integrity of your data. Set this to true only for testing and getting started with Atlas Data Federation.If omitted, defaults to false. | no |
| ------------- | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -- |
| urls          | array of strings or an empty array | One or more publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s. You can't specify URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s that require authentication.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | no |
| defaultFormat | string                             | Format that Data Federation defaults to if it encounters a file without an extension while querying the federated database instance store. The following values are valid:.json, .json.gz, .bson, .bson.gz, .avro, .avro.gz, .orc, .tsv, .tsv.gz, .csv, .csv.gz, .parquetIf omitted, Data Federation attempts to detect the file type by processing a few bytes of the file.If included, the specified format only applies to the URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s in the store.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | no |

**Oa:**

Atlas Online Archive Configuration

| Parameter   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required? |
| ----------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| createStore | string | Name of the new federated database instance store. The federated database instance store name must be unique.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | yes       |
| provider    | string | Name of the service where the data is stored. Value can be one of the following:s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.azure for Azure Blob Storage container.atlas for Atlas cluster.http for files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.dls:\<subtype> for online archives. Atlas Data Federation supports the following subtypes:aws - for data stored on AWS (Amazon Web Services). Value must be dls:aws.azure - for data stored on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Value must be dls:azure. | yes       |

| region | string | Region of your online archive. If you have multiple archives in different regions, you must add a store for each region to map data in that region to virtual databases and collections in federated database instances. | yes |
| ------ | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --- |

sections.heading: Output
sections.body:
The command prints the following output if it succeeds. If the command
fails, see [Troubleshoot Errors](adf-create-store-cmd-errors) for recommended solutions.

**S3:**

S3 Configuration

```json
 :copyable: false
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "<store-name>",
    "region": "<region-name>",
    "bucket": "<bucket-name>",
    "additionalStorageClasses": ["<storage-classes>"]
    "delimiter": "<delimiter>",
    "provider": "<storage-provider>"
  }
}
```

**Azure:**

Azure Configuration

```json
 :copyable: false
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "<store-name>",
    "region": "<region-name>",
    "serviceURL": "<service-account-URL>",
    "containerName": "<storage-container-name>"
    "delimiter": "<delimiter>",
    "provider": "<storage-provider>"
  }
}
```

**Gcp:**

GCP Configuration

```json
 :copyable: false
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "<store-name>",
    "region": "<region-name>",
    "bucket": "<storage-container-name>"
    "delimiter": "<delimiter>",
    "prefix": "<prefix>",
    "provider": "<storage-provider>"
  }
}
```

**Atlas:**

Atlas Configuration

```json
{
        "ok" : 1,
        "store" : {
                "name" : "<store-name>",
                "provider" : "<storage-provider>",
                "clusterName" : "<cluster-name>",
                "projectId" : "<project-id>",
                "readPreference" : {
                       "mode" : "<read-preference-mode>",
                       "tagSets" : [[{"name": "<name>", "value": "<value>"},...],...],
                       "maxStalenessSeconds" : <number-of-seconds>
                }
        }
}
```

**Http:**

HTTP Configuration

```json
{
        "ok" : 1,
        "store" : {
                "name" : "<store-name>",
                "provider" : "<storage-provider>",
                "allowInsecure" : true|false,
                "urls" : [
                        "<url>"
                ],
                "defaultFormat: "<file-extension>"
        }
}
```

**Oa:**

Atlas Online Archive Configuration

```json
{
  ok: 1,
  store: {
    name: '<store-name>',
    provider: 'dls:aws',
    region: '<online-archive-region>'
  }
}
```

sections.heading: Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `createStore` command to create a new
federated database instance store called `myStore`.

**S3:**

S3 Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "s3", region: "us-east-1", bucket: "my-bucket", "additionalStorageClasses" : ["STANDARD_IA","INTELLIGENT_TIERING"] })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "myStore",
    "region": "us-east-1",
    "bucket": "my-bucket",
    "additionalStorageClasses" : [
                        "STANDARD_IA",
                        "INTELLIGENT_TIERING"
                ],
    "delimiter": "/",
    "provider": "s3"
  }
}
```

**Azure:**

Azure Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "azure", region: "eastus2", serviceURL: "https://mystorage.blob.core.windows.net/", "containerName" : "my-data-container" })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "myStore",
    "region": "eastus2",
    "serviceURL": "https://mystorage.blob.core.windows.net/",
    "containerName" : "my-data-container",
    "delimiter": "/",
    "provider": "azure"
  }
}
```

**Gcp:**

GCP Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "azure", region: "us-central1", "bucket" : "my-data-container" })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  "ok": 1,
  "store": {
    "name": "myStore",
    "region": "us-central1",
    "bucket" : "my-data-container",
    "delimiter": "/",
    "provider": "gcp"
  }
}
```

**Atlas:**

Atlas Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "atlas", clusterName: "myTestCluster", projectId: "<project-id>", "readPreference": {"mode": "secondary", "tagSets": [[{"name": "provider", "value": "AWS" }, {"name": "region", "value": "US_EAST_1"}]], "maxStalenessSeconds": 120} })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
        "ok" : 1,
        "store" : {
                "name" : "myStore",
                "provider" : "atlas",
                "clusterName" : "myTestCluster",
                "projectId" : "<project-id>",
                "readPreference" : {
                        "mode" : "secondary",
                        "tagSets": [[{"name": "provider", "value": "AWS" }, {"name": "region", "value": "US_EAST_1"}]],
                        "maxStalenessSeconds" : 120
                }
        }
}
```

**Http:**

HTTP Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "http", urls: ["https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews","http://example.mongodb.com/sampleData"], allowInsecure: true, defaultFormat: ".json" })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
        "ok" : 1,
        "store" : {
                "name" : "http-store",
                "provider" : "http",
                "urls" : [
                        "https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews",
                        "http://example.mongodb.com/sampleData"
                ],
                "defaultFormat" : ".json"
                "allowInsecure" : true
        }
}
```

**Oa:**

Atlas Online Archive Configuration

**Input:**

```javascript
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "dls:aws", region: "us-east-1" })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  ok: 1,
  store: {
    name: 'myStore',
    provider: 'dls:aws',
    region: 'us-east-1'
  }
}
```

codeBlocks.content:
use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "s3", region: "us-east-1", bucket: "my-bucket", "additionalStorageClasses" : ["STANDARD_IA","INTELLIGENT_TIERING"] })

use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "azure", region: "eastus2", serviceURL: "https://mystorage.blob.core.windows.net/", "containerName" : "my-data-container" })

use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "azure", region: "us-central1", "bucket" : "my-data-container" })

use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "atlas", clusterName: "myTestCluster", projectId: "<project-id>", "readPreference": {"mode": "secondary", "tagSets": [[{"name": "provider", "value": "AWS" }, {"name": "region", "value": "US_EAST_1"}]], "maxStalenessSeconds": 120} })

use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "http", urls: ["https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews","http://example.mongodb.com/sampleData"], allowInsecure: true, defaultFormat: ".json" })

use sample
db.runCommand({ createStore: "myStore", provider: "dls:aws", region: "us-east-1" })

sections.heading: Troubleshoot Errors
sections.body:
If the command fails, it returns one of the following errors.

**Reason:**  a federated database instance store with the name specified in
`createStore` already exists.

```json
{
  "ok": 0,
  "errmsg": "store <store-name> already exists",
  "code": 2,
  "codeName": "BadValue"
}
```

**Solution:** Specify a unique name for the federated database instance store.

**Reason:** The specified `provider` isn't recognized.

```json
{
  "ok": 0,
  "errmsg": "unrecognized store provider <storage-provider>",
  "code": 2,
  "codeName": "BadValue"
}
```

**Solution:** Ensure that you specify a valid storage provider.

**S3:**

S3 Configuration

**Azure:**

Azure Configuration

**Gcp:**

GCP Configuration

**Atlas:**

Atlas Configuration

**Http:**

HTTP Configuration

**Reason:** One or more given URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s contain insecure HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme.

```json
{
        "ok" : 0,
        "errmsg" : "store 'httpStore': the insecure HTTP scheme is not supported by default - please add a 'allowInsecure: true' flag to the store or datasource to query from such URLs, correlationID = 16332c6eddf7b32776fde638",
        "code" : 72,
        "codeName" : "InvalidOptions"
}
```

**Solution:** Specify URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s with the secure HTTPS (Secure HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme. If the
specified URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s have the insecure HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme, set the `allowInsecure` flag to `true`. Note that setting the
`allowInsecure` flag to `true` leaves your data vulnerable to
man-in-the-middle attacks.

**Oa:**

Atlas Online Archive Configuration
